title: Fix server-side Sentry wiring (instrumentation.ts + withSentryConfig + release)
acceptance_criteria: src/instrumentation.ts exists at the project src/ level with an exported async register() function that conditionally imports sentry.server.config on NEXT_RUNTIME===nodejs and sentry.edge.config on NEXT_RUNTIME===edge
next.config.mjs adds experimental: { instrumentationHook: true } to nextConfig (required for Next.js 14.2.x to load instrumentation.ts)

next.config.mjs wraps its export with withSentryConfig (ESM import from @sentry/nextjs) with options: hideSourceMaps: true, disableLogger: true. tunnelRoute is NOT added (dropped to avoid proxy/rate-limit risk)
All three Sentry.init() calls (client/server/edge) add release: process.env.SENTRY_RELEASE to the init options
.env.example gains four new commented entries: SENTRY_RELEASE (git SHA / deploy tag), SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN (source map upload), SENTRY_ORG, SENTRY_PROJECT
npm run typecheck passes with zero new errors
npm run build succeeds (withSentryConfig wrapping does not break the standalone build)
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
